name: Chorizo (Mexican Sausage)
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Ground Red Chile, Mild OR Hot
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean the casings and rinse well with water. Pour vinegar through them. Set aside.
2. Using a food processor or the coarse blade of a meat grinder, grind the pork and fat. Add onions, garlic, vinegar, tequila, and seasonings. Adjust hotness to family preference.
3. Stuff the casings. Cut into 3-ft lengths, tie one end together. Use a funnel or filling tube to fill lengths at about 4-inch intervals with heavy thread.
4. Place on wax paper-covered cookie sheet. Let sit for about 2 hours, then refrigerate.
5. After a day, freeze unused portions in a week or two. Allow mixture to ripen for at least 8 hours before using.
Notes: If no food processor or grinder is available, purchase triple ground pork. Adjust the mildness or hotness of red chile for future recipes.
Freezing Hints: Mold sausage into 1/2-pound lumps and freeze between pieces of foil in a heavy plastic bag.
Maximum Recommended Freezer Storage: 3 Months
Makes 20 Sausages or 2 2/3 Pounds Bulk Sausage.
